goal: Tight-tolerance label-applicator pose canonical: a Franka arm equipped with an OgnSurfaceGripper vacuum cup picks a thin flat label (0.05 x 0.03 x 0.002 m Cube prim) from a feeder tray, transports it across the cell, and presses it onto a fixed workpiece (0.20 x 0.20 x 0.08 m Cube box) at a specified target pose with tight tolerances (±2 mm position, ±2° orientation). The label is too thin for a parallel-jaw gripper — surface_gripper (vacuum suction) is the only valid pickup modality. drop_targets is used to specify the exact world-space placement pose of the label centre on the workpiece top face, gripper_rotation pins the yaw alignment so the label is not rotated during placement, and a proximity sensor at the target pose triggers an arrival confirmation. get_world_transform is then called on the label and the workpiece to verify post-place pose-delta against the spec. Exercises vacuum-style label handling + tight-tolerance pose targeting as a pick_place pattern with destination_kind='fixture' (the workpiece IS the fixture). ONET role: labelling / packaging-line applicator operator.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-label-applicator-pose:

1) Distinctiveness: this canonical exercises tight-tolerance pose-critical placement of a thin flat label using vacuum pickup. Differs from CP-NEW-vacuum-gripper-sheet-pick (which is destination_kind='fixture' tray with 3 slots and loose 0.20m xy_tolerance) by enforcing a SINGLE specified target pose with ±2mm position and ±2° orientation tolerance. The workpiece is the fixture; the label centre must land at a designated target on the workpiece top face. This is the canonical exemplar for label-applicator and similar pose-critical pick_place patterns (packaging, marking, decal application).

2) Pattern_hint='pick_place' with destination_kind='fixture': pick_place is the correct shape (label moves from feeder to workpiece, success when label rests on workpiece). The workpiece (box) is the fixture, not a bin. Tight pose constraints are enforced via drop_targets (exact world xyz) and gripper_rotation (yaw alignment).

3) Scene geometry: Franka on 0.75m table at [0, 0, 0.75]. Feeder tray (LabelFeeder) at [+0.45, 0.0, 0.78] holding the label flat. Label (/World/Label_1) is a thin Cube 0.05 x 0.03 x 0.002m, centre z = feeder_top(0.80) + 0.001 = 0.801. Workpiece (/World/Workpiece) is a Cube 0.20 x 0.20 x 0.08m at [-0.35, 0.0, 0.79], top face at z = 0.83. Target label pose on the workpiece top: position=[-0.35, 0.0, 0.832] (centre + 2mm above for label thickness midpoint), orientation aligned with workpiece (yaw=0°). Feeder + workpiece both within Franka 0.855m reach (0.45m and 0.35m).

4) Why surface_gripper: a 2mm-thick label cannot be reliably grasped by Franka's parallel-jaw fingers (finger gap closes to 0mm at full close, but the precision needed to centre on a flat 2mm edge is impractical; the cup-based vacuum end-effector picks the label by surface contact on the top face, which is exactly how real label applicators work). grip_threshold=0.003m (3mm) tolerates the label thickness; force_limit=20.0N is well above the ~0.02kg label weight requirement.

5) Tight tolerance via drop_targets + gripper_rotation: setup_pick_place_controller with target_source='curobo' supports drop_targets (dict mapping cube_path → explicit [x,y,z]) and gripper_rotation (dict mapping cube_path → yaw_deg around world Z). We pin drop_targets={/World/Label_1: [-0.35, 0.0, 0.832]} so the label centre lands at the target spot, and gripper_rotation={/World/Label_1: 0.0} so the label is yaw-aligned with the workpiece. The ±2mm / ±2° tolerance is then verified post-place by get_world_transform on the label vs the target.

6) Arrival sensor at target: add_proximity_sensor at /World/PlaceArrivalSensor positioned at the target pose [-0.35, 0.0, 0.832] with a small detection volume [0.06, 0.04, 0.01] (slightly larger than the label's 0.05x0.03 footprint but tight in z so only a settled label triggers). This sensor's isaac_sensor:triggered flips True when the label arrives.

7) define_grasp_pose: invoked on (Franka, Label_1) with gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.001] (centre of label top face) and approach_direction=[0, 0, -1] (vertical descent). This pre-defines the suction grasp point so the controller can compute a clean approach trajectory.

8) Physics: CPU dynamics (enable_gpu_dynamics=False, MBP broadphase) for deterministic settling. Label has enableCCD=True to prevent tunnelling at high transit speed; sleepThreshold=0.0 keeps it active during placement. Label mass=0.02kg (typical paper label). Workpiece is static (no RigidBodyAPI, only CollisionAPI) — it's the fixture, world-fixed.

9) Why curobo: tight-tolerance placement requires deterministic Cartesian targeting at the drop point. cuRobo's collision-aware Cartesian planner places the EE at exactly drop_targets[label_path] with the yaw fixed by gripper_rotation, whereas native spline mode samples bin-top + 0.05m clearance and cannot enforce explicit yaw. RmpFlow lacks collision-aware obstacle avoidance, which matters because the EE must approach the workpiece face without clipping its sides.

10) Post-place verification: get_world_transform(/World/Label_1) reads the label's world pose after settle. The handler/diagnose pipeline compares this against simulate_args.target_pose [-0.35, 0.0, 0.832] and target_orient [1, 0, 0, 0] (identity quaternion) to verify the ±2mm / ±2° tolerance.

11) Failure modes (see failure_modes block) capture the dominant risks: label sticks to gripper after intended release (no air-blow modeled in OgnSurfaceGripper; the gripper opens by signal but adhesion is binary), label rotates during transport (CCD jitter on thin body), label placed on wrong face (workpiece is symmetric in xy; only top is the valid face), pose drift from off-centre vacuum grip (label tilts during transport if grasp point is not directly above centre of mass).
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")

# ── Work table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.9, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU determinism, CCD for thin label ───────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Franka Panda ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Surface gripper (vacuum cup on panda_hand) ───────────────────────────────
# grip_threshold=0.003m: vacuum cup seals onto label within 3 mm proximity.
# force_limit=20N: ample for ~0.02 kg paper label; low enough to be physically plausible.
surface_gripper(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    ee_link="/World/Franka/panda_hand",
    grip_threshold=0.003,
    force_limit=20.0,
    torque_limit=10.0,
    graph_path="/World/Franka/SuctionGraph",
)

# ── Label feeder tray (open-top container, label rests on its floor) ───────────────
# Feeder is a shallow tray on the table. Top floor surface at z=0.80.
create_bin(
    prim_path="/World/LabelFeeder",
    size=[0.10, 0.06, 0.02],
    position=[0.45, 0.0, 0.78],
    wall_thickness=0.003,
)

# ── Label (thin flat Cube prim) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Dimensions: 0.05 x 0.03 x 0.002 m. Centre on feeder floor (z=0.80 + 0.001 = 0.801).
# Default Cube prim has edge 1; scale halves give half-extents. 0.05m = scale 0.025; 0.002m = scale 0.001.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Label_1",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.45, 0.0, 0.801],
    scale=[0.025, 0.015, 0.001],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Label_1", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Label_1", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.02)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Label_1", attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Label_1", attr_name="physxRigidBody:enableCCD", value=True)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Label_1", material_name="rubber")

# Bulk-set retained for symmetry with other label-batch templates (single label here, but future-proof).
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Label_1"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:enableCCD",
    value=True,
)

# ── Workpiece (the fixture — label-application target box) ──────────────────────
# Static box at [-0.35, 0.0, 0.79], 0.20x0.20x0.08m, top face at z=0.83.
# No RigidBodyAPI — it is world-fixed (the fixture).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Workpiece",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[-0.35, 0.0, 0.79],
    scale=[0.10, 0.10, 0.04],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Workpiece", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Visual tint on workpiece so the label is visually distinguishable.
create_material(
    material_path="/World/Materials/WorkpieceGrey",
    shader_type="OmniPBR",
    diffuse_color=[0.55, 0.55, 0.58],
)
assign_material(
    prim_path="/World/Workpiece",
    material_path="/World/Materials/WorkpieceGrey",
)

# Label visual tint (white paper).
create_material(
    material_path="/World/Materials/LabelWhite",
    shader_type="OmniPBR",
    diffuse_color=[0.95, 0.95, 0.92],
)
assign_material(
    prim_path="/World/Label_1",
    material_path="/World/Materials/LabelWhite",
)

# ── Define grasp pose for label (top-down, centre of top face) ────────────────────
# gripper_offset = [0, 0, 0.001]: 1 mm above label centre (top face).
# approach_direction = [0, 0, -1]: pure vertical descent for vacuum contact.
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    object_path="/World/Label_1",
    gripper_offset=[0.0, 0.0, 0.001],
    approach_direction=[0.0, 0.0, -1.0],
)

# ── Arrival sensor at target pose on workpiece top face ─────────────────────────
# Tight detection volume: just larger than the label footprint, thin in z.
# Position is exactly at the target label-centre pose.
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/PlaceArrivalSensor",
    position=[-0.35, 0.0, 0.832],
    size=[0.06, 0.04, 0.01],
)

# ── cuRobo pick-place controller with explicit pose target + yaw alignment ─────────────
# drop_targets pins label centre to [-0.35, 0.0, 0.832] (workpiece top + 2mm clearance for
# half-label thickness 0.001m, leaving 1mm contact margin).
# gripper_rotation=0° holds the label yaw-aligned with the workpiece edges.
# approach_height=0.05: 50 mm clearance above target before vertical placement — small enough
# for tight tolerance, large enough that cuRobo can plan a collision-free descent.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=["/World/Label_1"],
    destination_path="/World/Workpiece",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Label_1": [-0.35, 0.0, 0.832],
    },
    gripper_rotation={
        "/World/Label_1": 0.0,
    },
    approach_height=0.05,
    planning_obstacles=[
        "/World/Table",
        "/World/LabelFeeder",
        "/World/Workpiece",
    ],
    sensor_path="/World/PlaceArrivalSensor",
)

# ── Post-place pose verification handles ───────────────────────────────────────
# These calls give the diagnose pipeline a snapshot of label and workpiece world poses
# so the ±2mm / ±2° tolerance can be checked downstream.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Label_1")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Workpiece")
code_template:
# ── Scene skeleton ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.9, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

surface_gripper(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    ee_link="{{primary_robot.path}}/panda_hand",
    grip_threshold=0.003,
    force_limit=20.0,
    torque_limit=10.0,
    graph_path="{{primary_robot.path}}/SuctionGraph",
)

create_bin(
    prim_path={{feeder.path}},
    size={{feeder.size}},
    position={{feeder.position}},
    wall_thickness=0.003,
)

create_prim(
    prim_path={{label.path}},
    prim_type="Cube",
    position={{label.position}},
    scale={{label.scale}},
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{label.path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{label.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{label.mass_kg}})
set_attribute(prim_path={{label.path}}, attr_name="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
set_attribute(prim_path={{label.path}}, attr_name="physxRigidBody:enableCCD", value=True)
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{label.path}}, material_name="rubber")

bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{label.path}}], attr="physxRigidBody:enableCCD", value=True)

create_prim(
    prim_path={{workpiece.path}},
    prim_type="Cube",
    position={{workpiece.position}},
    scale={{workpiece.scale}},
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/WorkpieceGrey", shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.55, 0.55, 0.58])
assign_material(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, material_path="/World/Materials/WorkpieceGrey")
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/LabelWhite", shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.95, 0.95, 0.92])
assign_material(prim_path={{label.path}}, material_path="/World/Materials/LabelWhite")

define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    object_path={{label.path}},
    gripper_offset=[0.0, 0.0, 0.001],
    approach_direction=[0.0, 0.0, -1.0],
)

add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path={{arrival_sensor.path}},
    position={{target_pose.position}},
    size={{arrival_sensor.size}},
)

setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=[{{label.path}}],
    destination_path={{workpiece.path}},
    drop_targets={ {{label.path}}: {{target_pose.position}} },
    gripper_rotation={ {{label.path}}: {{target_pose.yaw_deg}} },
    approach_height=0.05,
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", {{feeder.path}}, {{workpiece.path}}],
    sensor_path={{arrival_sensor.path}},
)

get_world_transform(prim_path={{label.path}})
get_world_transform(prim_path={{workpiece.path}})
failure_modes: Label sticks to gripper after release — OgnSurfaceGripper has no air-blow / active-detach modelling; once the close signal goes low the attachment FixedJoint is removed but the cup geometry can still drag the thin label on retreat. Mitigation: raise approach_height to 0.06 so the controller's retreat phase lifts straight up before lateral motion, minimising drag.

Label rotates during transport — the vacuum cup creates a single-point attachment at panda_hand; any wrist torque during transit rotates the label around the cup axis. Mitigation: gripper_rotation locks the yaw at the placement waypoint, but mid-trajectory rotation is not constrained. Increase home_target distance from feeder + workpiece line so the controller plans a smoother yaw trajectory, or use a lower max joint velocity.

Label placed on wrong face of workpiece — workpiece is symmetric in x and y; only the top face (+z) is the valid label substrate. drop_targets pins xy and z explicitly so this should not happen, but if drop_targets[z] is mis-set below workpiece.top (z=0.83) the controller would attempt to push the label INTO the workpiece. Always set drop_targets z = workpiece_top_z + half_label_thickness (here 0.83 + 0.001 = 0.831; we use 0.832 for a 1mm air-contact margin).

Pose drift from off-centre vacuum grip — if the panda_hand approach point is not directly above the label centre of mass, the label tilts when lifted (gravity torque around the cup axis). define_grasp_pose with gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.001] (centre of top face, 1mm above) ensures the cup contacts the geometric centre, which equals the centre of mass for a uniform-density cube. Asymmetric labels (real-world barcode + text) would need calibration.

approach_height too small for cuRobo — 0.05m clearance over a 0.83m workpiece top means cuRobo plans a tight descent through a narrow corridor between feeder (at +x) and workpiece (at -x). If cuRobo IK fails with 'no collision-free plan', raise approach_height to 0.08 (cost: ~0.5s longer cycle), or remove /World/Table from planning_obstacles since the workpiece sits above it and table collision is irrelevant during the placement descent.

Tolerance check uses world pose, not relative pose — get_world_transform returns the label's pose in /World frame. The diagnose pipeline computes pose_delta = world_label_pose - target_pose_position (both in world). If the workpiece itself were dynamic (not the case here — we made it static), the workpiece could shift and the world-frame check would still pass even if relative pose drifted. Static workpiece ensures world-frame check equals workpiece-relative check.
